baseBehaviors: lib/registry.qh:124-149 REGISTER_4/REGISTRY_PUSH — each REGISTER_* creates the singleton once, sets registered_id = "NS_id" (e.g. "WEP_BLASTER"), assigns the index field = registry COUNT at push time, i.e. DECLARATION ([[accumulate]] include) order, and maintains a first/last linked list.

lib/registry.qh:163-198 REGISTRY_SORT(id, skip) — heapsort of the registry array by strcmp(registered_id) (alphabetical by the registered-id string), SKIPPING the first `skip` entries which keep declaration order; runs in STATIC_INIT (before gameplay).

lib/registry.qh:213-254 REGISTRY_CHECK — MD4 hash over the ':'-joined registered_ids in FINAL (post-sort) order; server sends per-registry hash (Registry_send_all), client LOG_FATALF's on mismatch. Port analogue: Registry<T>.ContentHash() FNV-1a over RegistryName in sorted order (Registry.cs:57-63).

common/weapons/all.qh:353 `REGISTRY_SORT(Weapons, WEP_HARDCODED_IMPULSES + 1)` with all.inc:30 `#define WEP_HARDCODED_IMPULSES 19` — WEP_Null (slot 0) + the 19 weapons listed in all.inc:6-26 keep declaration order; only LATER-registered weapons are sorted alphabetically. all.qh:356-379 then assigns m_unique_impulse = 230.. in registry order (the order-matters consumer; impulse renumbering is what the skip protects).
common/items/all.qh:28 `REGISTRY_SORT(Items)` — Items fully alphabetical by "ITEM_*"; all.qh:15-26 CSQC snapshots the PRE-sort order (default_order_items) for category-ordered menu display.

common/monsters/all.qh:7-19 REGISTRY(Monsters, BITS(5)) + REGISTRY_CHECK, NO REGISTRY_SORT — declaration order with MON_Null at index 0. Same for turrets (common/turrets/all.qh:8-11, NullTurret at 121-125, TUR_FIRST=1 at 116) and vehicles (common/vehicles/all.qh:5-24, VEH_Null at 20-23, VEH_FIRST=1).

common/mutators/base.qh:184 REGISTRY(Mutators, BITS(7)) — no sort (declaration order); QC mutator HOOK order comes from the CallbackChain mechanism, not registry order (the port's MutatorActivation models that separately).

No registry-specific cvars exist in Base; the only boot-time cvar interaction the swap must preserve is the port-side Weapons.ConfigureAll() seeding every weapon's balance block from g_balance_* (QC autocvar WEP_CVAR reads, stock fallbacks when unset) — Registries.cs:125-129.
portState: GENERATOR EXISTS AND WORKS, BUT IS WIRED TO THE WRONG PROJECT AND NEVER CALLED (inert, not broken). Registry<Monster>/Registry<Turret>/Registry<Vehicle> DO exist — Registry<T> is the open generic (Framework/Registry.cs:29-70) and the Monsters/Turrets/Vehicles catalog accessors live at Gameplay/EntityClasses.cs:58-77; the base descriptor classes Monster/Turret/Vehicle at EntityClasses.cs:6/26/42 (all IRegistered, RegistryName => NetName).

Attribute declarations: WeaponAttribute/ItemAttribute/MutatorAttribute/GameTypeAttribute/MonsterAttribute in XonoticGodot.Common.Framework (Framework/Registry.cs:18-22); TurretAttribute in namespace XonoticGodot.Common.Gameplay (Gameplay/Turrets/TurretAI.cs:18, ns at :7); VehicleAttribute in namespace XonoticGodot.Common.Gameplay (Gameplay/Vehicles/VehicleCommon.cs:111, inside the ns block opened at :75 — note the file's first ns block :21 is Framework, don't be fooled).

Attribute usage census (all inside src/XonoticGodot.Common, top-level public classes, parameterless ctors — e.g. Blaster.cs:38): [Weapon]=24, [Item]=19, [Mutator]=41, [GameType]=20, [Monster]=5, [Turret]=12, [Vehicle]=4 → 125 expected registrations. ZERO attributed types in game/ or tests/ (greps empty), so generated registration in Common alone is complete coverage and the AppDomain-wide reflection scan finds nothing extra today.

Registry<T>.Sort() (Framework/Registry.cs:50-54) sorts the WHOLE list ordinal-by-RegistryName (=NetName, e.g. "blaster") and renumbers RegistryId — a known, accepted divergence from QC's skip-20 weapon sort; it is PORT-INTERNALLY consistent (RegistryId feeds WepSet bits Inventory.cs:21-23, ActiveWeaponId, deathtype ints, and wire fields ServerNet.cs:1332/1461, MinigameNetState.cs:24 — all computed identically on both legs of this port). IntegrationSmokeTests.cs:43-46 pins sequential-ids + ordinal-name-order; MenuWeaponOrderTests consumes RegistryId. T26 does not change this order.

risks: Dual-emission shadowing: if the host analyzer ref is NOT removed when Common's is added, the host compilation gains its own (empty) XonoticGodot.Common.Gameplay.GeneratedRegistrations that shadows Common's real one for any host-side caller (source beats metadata; CS0436) — must remove XonoticGodot.csproj:26 in the same change.

Fail-fast behavior change: reflection Bootstrap silently skips types whose ctor throws (Registries.cs:100-101 catch{continue}); generated `new X()` calls propagate ctor exceptions at boot. No current ctor reads cvars/services (spot-checked Blaster.cs:38, ArmorItem.cs; Configure() is the cvar reader and stays post-registration), so this is an improvement — but a latent throwing ctor added by another wave task would turn a silent missing-weapon into a boot crash.

Attribute/base mismatch becomes a compile error: reflection dispatches by runtime base type regardless of which attribute is used; the generator infers the catalog from the ATTRIBUTE name (GeneratorHelpers.InferCategory) and emits typed Register<T> calls — a [Item] on a Weapon subclass now fails the Common build (good fail-fast, but may surface during this wave's integration if another task mis-tags; the error message will be a CS1503 in GeneratedRegistrations.g.cs, slightly cryptic).

Suffix-based category matching (EndsWithOrdinal in CategoryFromSimpleName) — adding Turret/Vehicle/Monster is collision-safe today, but note "...ItemAttribute" suffix matching is greedy by design (tolerates subclassed markers); keep the match order irrelevant by ensuring no future attribute name ends with another category's suffix.

Reflection scan removal narrows discovery from ALL AppDomain assemblies to Common (+ explicitly-passed extraAssemblies): verified zero attributed types exist in game/, tests/, or other src projects today, but a future task adding a [Weapon] OUTSIDE Common would silently not register — mitigate by keeping the extraAssemblies reflection fallback and documenting that registrable content lives in Common (or wiring the analyzer into that project too).

QC weapon-order parity is NOT achieved by either path (port = full alphabetical NetName sort vs QC = Null+19-declaration-order then alphabetical "WEP_*") — pre-existing, port-internally consistent, pinned by IntegrationSmokeTests.cs:43-46; T26 must NOT 'fix' it in passing (it would renumber WepSet bits/deathtypes and break the order-pinning tests).

Tests project gains Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp 4.8.0 — Roslyn 4.8 requires the build SDK's compiler ≥ 4.8 (already true: the probe build ran the generator fine); pin the same version as the SourceGen csproj to avoid analyzer/driver version skew in tests.

Design-time tooling: ProjectReference-with-OutputItemType=Analyzer works in CLI, Godot editor builds, and IDE design-time builds, but open IDE sessions may need a restart/reload to pick up the new analyzer on Common (stale red squiggles on GeneratedRegistrations until then).
